question: Among the following descriptions of the network device virtualization technologies mentioned in the H3C CAS course, which is correct?
options: IRF is a 1:N virtualization technology that virtualizes one physical network device into multiple logical devices.
MDC is an N:1 virtualization technology that virtualizes multiple physical network devices into one device.
IRF is an N:1 virtualization technology that virtualizes multiple physical network devices into one network device.
MDC is used to consolidate physical servers into isolated VMs running on a hypervisor.
explanation: The course states that IRF is the network device N:1 virtualization technology that virtualizes multiple physical network devices into one network device, while MDC is the 1:N technology that virtualizes a single device into multiple logical ones.

question: Which of the following statements about server virtualization, as defined in the course, is correct?
options: Server virtualization keeps each physical server bound to a single OS instance to maximize per-host performance.
Server virtualization turns physical servers into logical resources with several isolated VMs running on them, improving resource utilization and simplifying management.
Server virtualization is implemented exclusively by container engines running directly on bare metal.
Server virtualization requires every VM to share the same GuestOS image to ensure compatibility.
explanation: The course defines server virtualization as making physical servers into logical resources with several isolated VMs running on them, leading to improved resource utilization, simplified management, server consolidation and greater agility.

question: Regarding the widely recognized definition of cloud computing cited in the course (referencing the NIST definition), which of the following statements is correct?
options: Cloud computing is a hardware procurement model that requires direct negotiation with service providers for every resource request.
Cloud computing is a service mode that allows users to easily obtain and release resources from a shared resource pool through the network without complicated interaction with service providers.
Cloud computing is a deployment model in which all resources must reside in a single public cloud operated by one provider.
Cloud computing is a virtualization technique limited to consolidating servers within a single data center.
explanation: The course quotes the widely recognized (NIST) definition: cloud computing is a service mode that allows users to easily obtain and release resources (network, server, storage, application, service) in a shared resource pool through the network without complicated communication and interaction with service providers.

question: Among the following descriptions of vMotion as presented in the CAS course, which is correct?
options: vMotion requires shared storage and supports only offline migration between hosts in the same cluster.
vMotion is limited to host migration only and does not support storage migration.
vMotion is the core foundation of HA, DRS, and DPM, and supports cross-host, cross-cluster, and cross-host-pool online/offline migration without relying on shared storage.
vMotion supports online migration only within a single CVK, and cannot cross CVM domains.
explanation: The course states vMotion needs no shared storage; supports host, storage and host+storage migration; supports cross-host, cross-cluster, cross-host-pool online/offline migration and cross-CVM long-distance migration; and is the core foundation of HA, DRS and DPM.

question: Which of the following descriptions of CAS native backup is correct?
options: CAS native backup has no additional cost to use the backup function, but does not support deduplication, compression, or encryption.
CAS native backup supports deduplication, compression and encryption, and is designed for very large backup workloads.
CAS native backup requires a third-party backup server such as AISHOO or DataVault.
CAS native backup supports VM, database and file backup natively without any limitation.
explanation: The course explicitly states CAS native backup has no additional cost, but its limitations are low backup rate (small scenarios), only VM backup (no database/file), and no deduplication, compression, or encryption features.

question: Which of the following are listed in the course as the five features of cloud computing?
options: Self-service
Flexible scheduling
Resource pooling
Network distribution
Continuous deduplication
Service measurable
explanation: The course's 'Five features of cloud computing' page enumerates exactly: Self-service, Flexible scheduling, Resource pooling, Network distribution, and Service measurable. 'Continuous deduplication' is not one of the listed features.

question: Which of the following statements about the three service modes of cloud computing presented in the course are correct?
options: IaaS provides physical machines and VM virtualization at the infrastructure layer, including computing, storage, network and computer room infrastructure.
PaaS provides middleware & runtime, database, and operating system on top of the IaaS layer.
SaaS delivers application software and data & information, with all underlying layers down to the computer room infrastructure managed by the provider.
PaaS delivers only computer room infrastructure and leaves the OS and database for the customer to install.
IaaS includes the application software layer such as CRM as part of its standard delivery.
explanation: The course's 'Three service modes' diagram shows IaaS covers physical/VM virtualization plus computing/storage/network/computer room; PaaS adds OS, database, middleware & runtime; SaaS adds the application software and data & information. Options D and E swap these layer boundaries.

question: Which of the following are listed in the course as the three storage virtualization implementation types?
options: Storage virtualization based on host
Storage virtualization based on storage device
Storage virtualization based on network device
Storage virtualization based on container engine
Storage virtualization based on hypervisor scheduler
explanation: The course's storage virtualization page lists exactly three types: based on host, based on storage device, and based on network device (the latter using a smart switch with built-in virtualization software).

question: Which of the following items are explicitly listed in the course as values of virtualization technology?
options: High-availability guarantee through virtualized HA capability for continuous service running
Hardware cost saving as the most intuitive expression of server virtualization
Power cost saving of up to 90% through virtualization integration and intelligent power management
Mandatory elimination of all physical servers in the data center
Automatic patching of all guest operating systems by the hypervisor
explanation: The 'Value of virtualization technology' slide lists high-availability guarantee, hardware cost saving (most intuitive expression of server virtualization), and power cost saving up to 90% via virtualization integration and intelligent power management, along with software/management cost savings and scalability/agility.

question: Among the following descriptions of DRX dynamic resource expansion in CAS, which is incorrect?
options: It supports automatically expanding or recycling service VMs based on performance parameters such as CPU/memory utilization and number of connections.
It supports quickly adding VMs in cloning and template deployment mode.
It supports recycling VMs in instant recycling and warm offline mode.
It requires manual creation of every additional VM by the administrator and does not support cloning or template-based deployment.
explanation: The DRX slide explicitly lists automatic expansion/recycling by performance parameters, quick VM addition via cloning/template, instant-recycling and warm-offline recycling modes, and LB device configuration on the interface. Option D contradicts the cloning/template capability.

question: Regarding the deployment models of cloud computing described in the course, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Public cloud is owned and managed by the cloud service provider, offering standardized cloud service products ordered on demand.
Private cloud is described as a system for internal office use by enterprise users.
Hybrid cloud combines self-use and leasing characteristics.
Public cloud is defined as a fully on-premises deployment exclusively for a single tenant.
explanation: The course defines public cloud as provider-owned/managed on-demand standardized services (not a single-tenant on-premises deployment), so option D is incorrect. Options A, B and C match the course descriptions of public, private and hybrid cloud.

question: Regarding the H3C cloud computing overall solution architecture shown in the course, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: CAS is positioned as the computing virtualization component of the H3C cloud computing solution.
ONEStor is positioned as the storage virtualization component of the H3C cloud computing solution.
SDN is positioned as the network virtualization component of the H3C cloud computing solution.
DOVE is shown as the dedicated network virtualization component in place of SDN in the H3C overall solution diagram.
explanation: The 'Overall solution of H3C cloud computing' diagram pairs CAS (computing virtualization), SDN (network virtualization), and ONEStor (storage virtualization), all running on the UIS Unified Infrastructure System. DOVE does not appear in the H3C diagram.

question: Among the following descriptions of the FBWC (Flash-Based Write Cache) protection mechanism of the RAID card, which is correct?
options: It uses battery power that generally lasts for 72 hours, within which the server must be powered on to input cached data to the hard disk.
It uses the flash to store cached data and writes the cached data to the flash for storage when the power is off; the process is permanent and effective without time limit.
It writes the system write request directly to the hard disk, providing high security and low performance.
It is identical to the BBWC mechanism except that it uses lithium-ion batteries instead of NiCd batteries.
explanation: Per the RAID card cache battery section, FBWC uses flash to persist cached data when power is off and the write completes in a few seconds; it is permanent and effective without time limit, distinguishing it from BBWC which only lasts 72 hours.

question: Which of the following statements about the iSCSI protocol port and encapsulation is correct?
options: iSCSI PDUs are encapsulated directly into IP packets, bypassing TCP for lower latency.
The iSCSI port is 3260, and each PDU is encapsulated in a TCP packet then layered for Ethernet transmission.
The iSCSI port is 3268, and iSCSI packets are carried over UDP for orderly transmission.
iSCSI uses the FCP protocol over TCP to transmit SCSI CDBs across the IP network.
explanation: The iSCSI packet format section specifies the iSCSI port 3260 and that each PDU containing a SCSI CDB is encapsulated in a TCP packet, then encapsulated layer by layer for transmission in the Ethernet.

question: Among the following descriptions of RAID 5 capacity and fault tolerance, which is correct?
options: RAID 5 requires a minimum of 2 hard disks and tolerates one disk failure.
RAID 5 requires a minimum of 3 hard disks, has an effective capacity of (n-1) x c, and tolerates one disk failure.
RAID 5 requires a minimum of 4 hard disks and stores parity on a single dedicated disk.
RAID 5 requires a minimum of 3 hard disks and tolerates two simultaneous disk failures.
explanation: The RAID comparison table lists RAID 5 minimal configuration as 3, disk utilization rate as (N-1)/N, with parity distributed across all disks; it provides fault tolerance for one failed disk.

question: Regarding the DHCP protocol's transport encapsulation, which of the following statements is correct?
options: DHCP packets are encapsulated using TCP; the server listens on port 67 and the client on port 68.
DHCP packets are encapsulated using UDP; the server listens on port 67 and the client on port 68.
DHCP packets are encapsulated using UDP; the server listens on port 68 and the client on port 67.
DHCP packets are encapsulated using UDP; both server and client use port 547.
explanation: The Introduction to DHCP states DHCP packets are encapsulated by using UDP; the server intercepts on port 67 and the client uses port 68.

question: Which of the following statements about the link aggregation operation key is correct?
options: The operation key is manually configured by the administrator on the aggregation interface.
The operation key is a configuration combination automatically generated by the aggregation control according to some configurations of member ports during link aggregation.
The operation key is exchanged via LACPDUs and is therefore only present in static aggregation mode.
The operation key uniquely identifies the link aggregation group on Layer 3 routes.
explanation: The Related concepts of link aggregation page defines the operation key as a configuration combination automatically generated by the aggregation control according to configurations of member ports during link aggregation.

question: Among the following descriptions of the FC FC-2 layer, which is correct?
options: FC-2 defines the physical port features including the transmission medium, the transmit end and the receive end.
FC-2 specifies the transmission mechanisms including the frame format and information exchange between nodes.
FC-2 provides the protocol mapping relationship between low-level FC and high-level protocols such as IP or SCSI.
FC-2 defines serial decoding, encoding, and error control with a specific link control function.
explanation: Per the FC protocol layer description, FC-2 (Data Distribution Layer) specifies transmission mechanisms including the frame format and information exchange between nodes. Physical interface is FC-0, byte coding is FC-1, and high-level protocol mapping is FC-4.

question: Regarding the DAS storage architecture, which of the following statements is correct?
options: DAS connects the storage device to the existing network and provides file services via CIFS/NFS protocols.
DAS storage devices are directly connected to the server through a cable (usually a SCSI interface cable), taking the server as the center.
DAS uses the iSCSI protocol over Ethernet to access data in block mode.
DAS uses FC switches and the FCP protocol to access data in block mode.
explanation: The Storage architecture--DAS slide states DAS storage devices are directly connected to the server through a cable (usually a SCSI interface cable), with I/O requests sent directly to the storage device, taking the server as the center.

question: Which of the following descriptions of the NTP working modes is correct?
options: Server and peer modes do not require specifying an address, since clock synchronization is auto-discovered.
Broadcast and multicast modes are used when the IP address of the server or peer cannot be determined, or when there are many devices to be synchronized.
Broadcast mode requires the server and peer addresses to be statically configured at both ends.
Client/server mode is the only mode that supports password verification of NTP.
explanation: The Working modes of NTP slide states broadcast and multicast modes are used when the IP address of the server or peer cannot be determined, or there are many devices to be synchronized on the network.

question: Among the following descriptions of the basic ACL, which is correct?
options: The basic ACL is identified by serial numbers in the range 3000 to 3999.
The basic ACL only formulates rules based on information about the source IP address of the packet.
The basic ACL formulates rules based on Layer 3 and Layer 4 information of the packet.
The basic ACL is based on user-defined character strings such as the packet header and the IP header.
explanation: The Basic ACL slide states the basic ACL only formulates rules based on information about the source IP address of the packet, and can be used to restrict host-to-host mutual access. Per the ACL identification table, the basic ACL serial range is 2000-2999, not 3000-3999.

question: Which of the following statements about the QoS LR (Line Rate) function is correct?
options: The LR can be applied to both incoming and outgoing directions of the interface.
The LR is located at the network layer and only takes effect for IP packets.
The LR introduces a queue buffer mechanism in a token bucket, reducing the packet loss rate and smoothing traffic, but also increasing the delay.
QoS can create extra bandwidth through the LR mechanism when the link is congested.
explanation: The QoS rate limit page states LR introduces a queue buffer mechanism in a token bucket, thereby reducing the packet loss rate, smoothing the traffic, but also increasing the delay. LR is located at the link layer and applied only to the outgoing direction; QoS cannot create bandwidth.

question: Which of the following statements about RAID levels are correct?
options: RAID 0 requires at least 2 hard disks and has no fault tolerance.
The disk capacity utilization of RAID 1 is 50%.
RAID 6 requires at least 4 hard disks and allows two disks to fail simultaneously.
RAID 10 has a minimum configuration of 3 hard disks and a utilization of (N-1)/N.
In comparison among RAID 0/1/5/6, RAID 0 has the highest performance and the worst security.
explanation: Per the RAID comparison table and the end-of-chapter question: RAID 0 has no fault tolerance with at least 2 disks needed for striping; RAID 1 utilization 50%; RAID 6 minimal 4 with two parity disks; RAID 0 has highest performance and worst security. RAID 10's minimum is 4 with 50% utilization, not 3 with (N-1)/N.

question: Which of the following statements about storage multi-path are correct?
options: Implementation methods of multi-path include an Active-Active mode and an Active-Passive mode.
If the host has two HBA ports and the storage side has two controllers, with each HBA reaching each controller, a total of four storage paths can be found on the host side.
Without multi-path software, one LUN may be identified as multiple LUNs on the host side.
Multi-path can improve reliability and balance load, but cannot increase bandwidth.
Multi-path only works with FC SAN and is not supported by IP SAN.
explanation: The multi-path slides list Active-Active and Active-Passive implementations, the 4-path example (HBA01/02 to Controller 1/2), and that without multi-path software the host sees one LUN as multiple. Functions of multi-path explicitly include 'Increase bandwidth', so option D is wrong.

question: Which of the following statements about the storage thin volume are correct?
options: The thin volume allows storage to be over-allocated to hosts.
When the thin volume is mirrored and cloned within a storage and replicated between storages, only data actually used needs to be synchronized, saving CPU, disk, and bandwidth overhead.
When the capacity in the storage pool is used up and new resources are not added in time, the thin volume cannot expand normally and data cannot be written.
A thin volume can dynamically expand its capacity up to the volume capacity specified at creation time.
The thin volume's final expanded capacity is unlimited, independent of the initial specified capacity.
explanation: The thin provisioning slides confirm over-allocation, replication savings for actually used data only, the storage pool exhaustion failure mode, and the cap at the volume capacity specified when the volume is created. Option E directly contradicts the stated cap.

question: Which of the following statements about the RAID card cache and cache battery are correct?
options: The RAID card supports the write through and the write back modes; write through writes directly to the hard disk with high security and low performance.
The cache battery is used to ensure security of data in the cache of the RAID card when the server is powered off.
The BBWC (Battery-Backed Write Cache) battery power generally lasts for 72 hours.
Write back mode writes the system write request directly to the hard disk first, then to the cache.
explanation: The RAID card slides confirm both write modes with the stated security/performance trade-off, the cache battery's purpose at power-off, and the BBWC 72-hour spec. Option D inverts the write back definition (write back first stores in cache, then writes back to disk).

question: Which of the following statements about static and dynamic link aggregation modes are correct?
options: In static aggregation mode, the port does not exchange information with the peer device.
In dynamic aggregation mode, the LACP protocol is automatically enabled to exchange LACP packets with the peer device.
In static aggregation mode, the reference port is selected according to information of the local device.
Dynamic aggregation creates and deletes the aggregation group without any user command.
explanation: The Link aggregation mode slide lists exactly: static does not exchange info, picks the reference port from local info, and creates/deletes the static group by user command; dynamic enables LACP. Both static and dynamic require a user command for create/delete, so D is wrong.

question: Which of the following are valid hot spare disk types of RAID?
options: Global hot spare disk, which serves all RAID groups in the array.
Dedicated hot spare disk, which only serves a specific RAID group.
Distributed hot spare disk, which spreads spare capacity across all disks in the group.
Roaming hot spare disk, which moves between RAID groups based on workload.
Tiered hot spare disk, which is shared between SAS and SATA pools.
explanation: The Hot spare disk of RAID page lists exactly two types: Global hot spare disk (serves all RAID groups in the array) and Dedicated hot spare disk (only serves a specific RAID group). The other three are invented terms.

question: Which of the following statements about COW (Copy On Write) snapshots are correct?
options: The source volume is kept up-to-date during snapshot operations.
On a write to the source, current data at the target address is first read and stored to a dedicated space with an index, then the new write is performed.
The read path of the source volume is basically not affected.
The source volume is frozen during snapshot operations and all writes are logged.
explanation: Per the Comparison of COW and ROW table, COW keeps the source volume up-to-date, reads the original block and indexes it before overwriting, and leaves the source's read path basically unaffected. Freezing the source and logging writes describes ROW, not COW.

question: Among the following statements about the hard disk, which is incorrect?
options: Hard disks are classified into SATA, SAS, FC, and SSD hard disks according to their types.
Hard disks are classified into 3.5-inch, 2.5-inch, and 1.8-inch according to their sizes.
Hard disks are classified into 15000 rpm, 10000 rpm, and 7200 rpm according to rotational speeds.
Compared with the 10K rpm hard disk, the 15K rpm hard disk has higher performance and a longer service life.
explanation: The hard disk classification slides list types, sizes, and rotational speeds exactly as in A/B/C. The end-of-chapter question explicitly flags option D as false: higher rotational speed gives higher performance but does not give a longer service life.

question: Regarding the iSCSI protocol layers, which of the following statement is wrong?
options: The SCSI layer establishes a SCSI CDB based on application requests and passes it to the iSCSI layer.
The iSCSI layer encapsulates the SCSI CDB so that it can be transmitted over the network based on TCP/IP.
The TCP layer provides end-to-end transparent and reliable transmission.
The IP layer encapsulates the iSCSI PDU directly into the data link frame, bypassing TCP.
explanation: The iSCSI initiator-target model lists SCSI -> iSCSI -> TCP -> IP -> Link layers. The IP layer performs routing and forwarding on IP packets; it does not bypass TCP. Each PDU is encapsulated in a TCP packet, not directly in a link-layer frame.

question: Among the following statements about link aggregation, which is incorrect?
options: Link aggregation is divided into static link aggregation and dynamic link aggregation.
If a server is cooperated with a switch, configuring dynamic aggregation on the server and static aggregation on the switch will still negotiate successfully.
Link aggregation can increase link network bandwidth and increase link reliability.
Link aggregation is used to aggregate multiple physical links together to form a logical link.
explanation: Per the link aggregation mode definitions, static mode does not exchange information with the peer while dynamic mode requires LACP exchange; the two cannot negotiate successfully across a mismatch. The end-of-chapter question marks B as false.

question: Regarding storage protocols, which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: FC SAN uses the FCP protocol over a Fibre Channel network to access data in block mode.
IP SAN uses the iSCSI protocol over Ethernet to access data in block mode.
NAS supports the network file sharing protocols CIFS and NFS.
iSCSI encapsulates SCSI commands using the FCP protocol and transmits them over Ethernet.
The FC protocol is logically a one-way broadcast data channel for high-performance storage.
explanation: Per the network-based SCSI page, iSCSI encapsulates the SCSI command by using TCP/IP, not FCP. The FC protocol is logically a two-way point-to-point serial data channel, not a one-way broadcast channel. Options A, B, C are exact restatements from the SAN/NAS slides.

question: Among the following statements about the FC port types, which are wrong?
options: N_Port is a node port directly connected to the Fabric.
F_Port is a Fabric port on the switch connected to N_Port.
E_Port is the expansion port used to connect switch to switch.
U_Port is a dedicated uplink port used only for connections to FC routers.
FCoE_Port is a general port that can be changed to E or F.
explanation: The FC port type slide lists N_Port, F_Port, E_Port, and U_Port (general port that can be changed to E or F). Option D mis-defines U_Port; option E invents the term FCoE_Port and steals the U_Port definition.

question: Regarding the comparison of storage protocols (SCSI / FC / iSCSI), which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: The SCSI interface technology is parallel; the SCSI current maximum rate is 320 MB/s.
The FC protocol uses a serial interface; FC compatibility is rated as poor.
The iSCSI protocol uses an Ethernet switch or router; the iSCSI current maximum rate is 20 Gb/s.
FC uses Ethernet switches and standard NICs as adapters.
SCSI compatibility is rated as poor due to the parallel bus design.
explanation: The Comparison of storage protocols table shows: SCSI parallel 320 MB/s (A correct); FC serial with FC switches and FC HBA, compatibility poor (B correct, D wrong because FC needs FC switches and FC HBAs, not Ethernet); iSCSI Ethernet switch/router 20 Gb/s (C correct); SCSI compatibility is rated Good in the table, not poor (E wrong).

question: Among the following statements about VLAN port types on the vSwitch, which is incorrect?
options: An Access port carries a single PVID and is used to connect to a VM.
A Trunk port can carry multiple VLAN tags such as PVID:10,20.
VLAN can be used to isolate broadcast storms within a Layer 2 switch.
An Access port exchanges LACP packets with the peer device to negotiate VLAN membership.
explanation: Per the Port type of VLAN link diagram, Access ports carry a single PVID to a VM and Trunk ports carry multiple PVIDs (e.g., PVID:10,20); VLAN isolates broadcast storms. LACP belongs to dynamic link aggregation, not Access-port VLAN negotiation.

question: The H3C CAS virtualization platform deployment process is divided into three main steps. The following are the three main steps in the correct order:
options: Cluster installation deployment; Initial deployment planning; VM configuration deployment
Initial deployment planning; Cluster installation deployment; VM configuration deployment
Initial deployment planning; VM configuration deployment; Cluster installation deployment
Cluster installation deployment; VM configuration deployment; Platform initialization
explanation: The introduction explicitly lists three main steps: 1. Initial deployment planning; 2. Cluster installation deployment; 3. VM configuration deployment.

question: Regarding the hardware-assisted virtualization requirements of H3C CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: x86 servers must support Intel-VT or AMD-V, and ARM servers must support ARM VHE virtualization extension
All servers must support Intel-VT regardless of CPU architecture
ARM servers require AMD-V emulation while x86 servers require Intel-VT only
Only the management server CPU must support hardware-assisted virtualization
explanation: The PDF states H3C CAS is a hardware-assisted virtualization system requiring Intel-VT or AMD-V on x86, and ARM VHE virtualization extension on ARM-based servers.

question: When installing the operating system, on the SOFTWARE SELECTION page, which component should be ticked to install a management server?
options: CVK
CVM-Chinese or CVM-English
CIC-Manager
CAStools
explanation: To install the management server, tick the CVM-Chinese or CVM-English option; the installer selects CVK by default for service servers.

question: Regarding the default login credentials of the H3C CAS management platform after installation, which is correct?
options: User name admin, password admin@1234
User name root, password Cloud@1234
User name admin, password Cloud@1234
User name administrator, password H3C@1234
explanation: The PDF states the default user name and password for the H3C CAS login interface are admin/Cloud@1234.

question: When installing the system on a CVK service host with manual partitioning, which of the following mount points must be added?
options: /, /boot/efi, /boot, swap, /vms, /var/log
/, /boot, /home, /opt, swap, /vms
/, /boot/efi, /cvm, /cvk, swap, /var
/, /boot, /etc/cas, /vms, swap, /tmp
explanation: The manual partition step instructs adding /, /boot/efi, /boot, swap, /vms, and /var/log partitions in turn.

question: Regarding NTP time configuration in a CAS cluster, which statement is correct?
options: Each CVK host must independently sync to an external NTP pool, ignoring the CVM
NTP service is disabled by default and must be enabled manually after installation
It is recommended to use the CVM host as the NTP server and other CVK hosts as NTP clients; in a hot standby environment, the virtual IP is used
Only the management network gateway can act as the NTP server for CVK hosts
explanation: The PDF states NTP is enabled by default after install, and recommends CVM as NTP server with CVK as NTP clients; virtual IPs are used in hot standby environments.

question: Regarding the difference between 'Cloned as Template' and 'Convert to Template' on a powered-off VM, which statement is correct?
options: Both operations delete the original VM after template creation
Cloned as Template keeps the original VM; Convert to Template converts the original VM into a template and the original VM is no longer retained
Cloned as Template creates a thin-provisioned copy while Convert to Template requires the VM to be running
Convert to Template retains the original VM as a snapshot while Cloned as Template removes the original
explanation: Per PDF: Cloned as Template retains the original VM; Convert to Template converts the original VM into the template and the original is not retained.

question: Which of the following statements about CAS host pool, cluster, and shared file system planning are correct?
options: It is recommended that one host pool includes less than four HA clusters
Each HA cluster includes less than 32 CVK hosts
A shared file system storage volume should be less than 64TB
A shared file system can be used simultaneously by at most 64 hosts in any cluster
It is recommended that each cluster includes less than 10 shared file systems
explanation: The PDF specifies <4 HA clusters per host pool, <32 CVK hosts per HA cluster, storage volume <64TB, <10 shared file systems per cluster, and a shared file system can be simultaneously used by at most 32 hosts (not 64) and only in one cluster.

question: Which of the following are configuration prerequisites for setting up a stateful failover (hot standby) system in H3C CAS?
options: The newly installed system has not been used as a CVK host and there are no managed CVK hosts in the management platform
The two hosts must use automatic partitioning to install the same version of the CVM system
At least 30G of space is allocated to the /vms partition
Sizes of the system disks of the two hosts must be the same
The storage pool must include at least three custom pools beyond isopool and defaultpool
explanation: PDF prerequisites include: new system not used as CVK, automatic partitioning with same CVM version, at least 30G /vms, identical system disk sizes. The storage pool MUST keep system default (only isopool and defaultpool) - adding pools is forbidden, so option E is wrong.

question: Which of the following statements about CAS hot standby arbitration methods are correct?
options: Advanced arbitration designates a CVK host as the quorum host that determines the role of each node
Simple ping specifies two quorum IP addresses and the node that fails to ping any becomes the backup
Advanced arbitration is recommended for VM deployment scenarios
Simple ping is recommended for physical server deployment scenarios
Both arbitration methods require an external dedicated arbitrator appliance
explanation: PDF describes advanced arbitration via a CVK quorum host and simple ping via two quorum IPs. Advanced arbitration is recommended for both VM deployment and physical server deployment (simple ping is the 'not recommended' option in both cases). No external appliance is required.

question: Which of the following are correct statements about CAS storage and disk planning?
options: If the server is equipped with two local disks, configure them in RAID 1
If the server uses more than two local disks, configure them in RAID 5
External storage (IP SAN/FC SAN) is recommended with RAID 5 or RAID 6
Thin provisioning is recommended when creating storage volumes
In a host pool with only two CVK hosts, hardware-assisted locking is required and the storage server must support the CAW feature
explanation: PDF recommends RAID1 for two local disks, RAID5 for >2 local disks, RAID5/6 for external storage. Thick (Full) Provisioning is recommended for storage volumes, not thin. In a 2-CVK host pool, hardware-assisted locking and CAW-support storage are required.

question: Which of the following are correct CAS performance specifications?
options: The maximum number of hosts supported by a single CVM is 256
The maximum number of virtual machines supported by a single CVM is 5000
The maximum number of computing servers in a single HA shared file system cluster is 32
The maximum number of K8S cluster control nodes is 3
The maximum number of mounted LUNs per physical host is 256
explanation: PDF confirms 256 hosts per CVM, 5000 VMs per CVM, 32 compute servers per HA shared file system cluster, and 3 K8S control nodes. Maximum mounted LUNs per physical host is 128, not 256.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS management server hardware requirements, which is incorrect?
options: If the management server does not join the host pool and runs no VMs, the CPU is not required to support virtualization
For scale of Server <50 and VM <1000, 16 CPU cores, 32 GB memory and 600 G storage are recommended with physical machine deployment
For scale of Server 256 to 512 and VM >5000, 32 CPU cores, 256 G memory and two SSD disk sets (960 G) RAID 1 are recommended
The management server CPU must always support Intel-VT or AMD-V regardless of whether it joins the host pool
explanation: PDF explicitly states that if the management server does not join the host pool and runs no VMs, CPU virtualization support is NOT required - option D contradicts this.

question: Regarding the IP SAN networking plan for CAS, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: The management network is used by CVM to access the CAS cluster and is also used for CVM-to-CVK communication
The storage network is used when CVK accesses IP SAN storage and uses two NICs in link aggregation
The service network is the network accessed by VM services with two NICs in link aggregation
If migration and backup networks are not specified, they default to using the storage network; the storage network is thus recommended to be 10 GB
explanation: The PDF note states that if migration and backup networks are not specified, they share the MANAGEMENT network (not storage), and therefore the management network is recommended to be 10 GB.

question: Regarding the CAS license registration process, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Obtain the host information file host.info from the Product Registration dialog in the CAS login interface
Apply for the activation file (.lic) on H3C's website under Support - Online Help - License Service - Register the First Time
Upload the activation file in the CAS login interface Product Registration/Local License to register the license
The host information file is named host.cfg and is generated automatically inside /etc/cvm at first boot
explanation: The host information file is named host.info (not host.cfg) and is downloaded via the CAS Product Registration dialog box - there is no auto-generated /etc/cvm/host.cfg path in the PDF.

question: Regarding VM computing and network resource planning in CAS, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Different priorities (high, medium, low) are assigned to different VM levels to ensure resources are preferentially allocated to important VMs in case of resource preemption
After the VM starts it occupies memory resources, so it is necessary to reserve 1/3 of free memory resources
Older Linux systems such as RHEL 4.x are recommended to use the E1000 network interface card
The Windows operating system supports the high-speed network interface card by default without any additional driver
explanation: PDF says Windows does NOT support the high-speed NIC by default - the high-speed network interface card driver needs to be loaded through CAStools.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS cluster installation and initialization process, which steps are incorrect?
options: Install the CVM/CVK operating system
Configure hot standby (optional) before registering the license
Install HDM agent on every CVK host immediately after the CVM/CVK operating system installation
Create the host pool/cluster, add the host, configure the vSwitch and the storage pool
Run a mandatory iLO firmware flash from the CAS management platform before logging in
explanation: The documented order is: Install CVM/CVK OS, set time, log in to CAS, configure hot standby (optional), register license, create host pool/cluster, add host, configure vSwitch, configure storage pool. There is no HDM agent install step and no mandatory iLO firmware flash step in the PDF.

question: Regarding CAStools installation on a VM, which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: After the VM is installed, the H3C CAS platform cannot obtain VM CPU and memory utilization information without CAStools
For Linux VMs, run ./CAS_tools_install.sh after mounting the CD-ROM and entering the Linux directory of CAStools
For Windows VMs, after CAStools setup completes, the high-speed NIC driver is also loaded
After CAStools is installed, the CD-ROM should remain permanently connected for ongoing monitoring
Installing CAStools on the Linux VM only requires executing the rpm -ivh castools.rpm command from /root
explanation: PDF says after CAStools installation, click Disconnect on the CD-ROM page (not keep permanently connected), and the Linux install command is ./CAS_tools_install.sh from the Linux directory of CAStools, not an rpm install from /root.

question: In the hierarchical management model of H3C CAS cloud resources, which of the following correctly describes the relationship between host pool, cluster, and host?
options: A cluster is a collection of host pools, and a host can be added only to a cluster
A host pool is a collection of a series of hosts and clusters, and a host can be added to the host pool or the cluster
A host pool is a sub-unit of a cluster, and hosts can be added only to the host pool
Host pools and clusters are peer collections; a host must belong to exactly one host pool
explanation: Per the PDF, a host pool is a collection of a series of hosts and clusters, and the host can be added to the host pool or the cluster. The cluster is a computing resource collection composed of physical hosts and VMs.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CPU host-matching mode (host-mode) in CAS, which is correct?
options: The QEMU emulated CPU uses the type QEMU64, providing the highest compatibility but no aes-encryption support
The host CPU model and most functions are transparently passed through to the VM, providing optimal performance
The QEMU emulated CPU is close to the host CPU and is as close as possible to the host CPU in performance, with poorer migration compatibility
Time slices for all VMs are equalized so that the host CPU model is hidden entirely from the guest
explanation: Host matching mode: the QEMU emulated CPU is close to the host CPU, is as close as possible to the host CPU in performance, and is poorer in migration compatibility. QEMU64 describes Compatibility mode and pass-through describes Straight-through mode.

question: Which of the following statements about the balloon driver in CAS virtual memory management is correct?
options: It slices host memory in 4 KB pages and replaces duplicate pages with pointers in a global hash table
It swaps VM memory pages that have not been used for a long time to a disk file and maps them back when accessed
It is installed in the H3C CAStools in the guest OS to reclaim memory and release free memory in the VM to another VM that needs more memory on the same host
It is a hypervisor-level page-sharing scheme provided by VMware ESXi and adopted unchanged by CAS
explanation: The PDF describes the balloon driver as installed in CAStools in the guest OS to reclaim memory and, through balloon expansion, release free memory in the VM to another VM needing more memory on the same host. Option A describes transparent page sharing; B describes memory swapping.

question: Regarding the virtual disk cache mode "Level 2 virtual cache (writeback)" in CAS, which of the following is correct?
options: Both the host cache and the virtual disk write cache are shut down; data is written directly to the physical disk
Before being written to disk, data is first written to the cache; once written successfully to the cache the write is considered successful and is not immediately synchronized to disk
The host cache is shut down and data is read and written only from the virtual disk cache
Only the read cache exists; the virtual disk write cache is shut down so writes go straight to disk
explanation: PDF: Level 2 virtual cache (writeback): before written to the disk, data is first written to the cache; after successfully written to the cache the write is considered successful, and is not immediately synchronized to disk. A is directsync, C is none, D is writethrough.

question: Which of the following correctly describes the three-level mirror (image) structure of a VM in CAS?
options: Level 1 image is read-write and shared by all VMs; Level 2 and Level 3 are read-only per VM
VMs share a read-only Level 1 image file; each VM has its own read-write Level 2 and Level 3 image files, and data written to the VM is stored only to Level 3
Level 1, Level 2, and Level 3 image files are all per-VM read-write copies linked by pointers
Data written to the VM is stored only to the Level 1 image file; Level 2 and Level 3 hold only metadata
explanation: PDF: VMs share a read-only Level 1 image file. The VM has its own read-write Level 2 and Level 3 image files. Data written to the VM is stored only to Level 3 image files. Level 2 image files are used for backup.

question: Which of the following best describes the differential backup type as defined in the CAS VM backup function?
options: Backing up only data that has changed since the last backup of any type
Backing up all data and applications at a specific time point with no reference to any prior backup
Backing up, based on the full backup, only data that has changed since the last full backup
Backing up only the dirty bitmap held in memory, without copying any data blocks
explanation: PDF: The differential backup means backing up based on the full backup, and only backing up data that has changed since the last full backup. Option A is incremental, B is full, D describes part of the CBT mechanism.

question: Regarding the relationship between vFirewall (vFW) and ACL policies on a CAS VM, which of the following is correct?
options: If both a vFirewall and an ACL are configured for a VM, the ACL takes effect first
vFirewalls and ACLs are mutually exclusive; if both are configured for a VM, the vFirewall takes effect
vFirewall rules and ACL rules are merged and both apply simultaneously
vFirewalls only filter inbound traffic, while ACLs only filter outbound traffic, so they cannot conflict
explanation: PDF: vFirewalls and ACLs are mutually exclusive. If both a vFirewall and an ACL are configured for a VM, the vFirewall takes effect.

question: Which of the following correctly describes online VM migration prerequisites and behavior in CAS?
options: Online migration requires that the VM be shut down before the host change is initiated
Online migration means migrating the VM in a running state, and the image file must be stored in shared storage
Online migration transfers the image file across the management network from local storage to local storage
Online migration is supported only when the source and target hosts use different CPU generations
explanation: PDF: Online migration means migrating the VM in a running state; the image file must be stored in the shared storage. Memory data is transferred through the management network.

question: Which of the following correctly describes the storage path priority that CAS automatically selects when adding a VM?
options: Local > Shared File System > RBD
Shared File System > Local > RBD
RBD > Shared File System > Local
Local > RBD > Shared File System
explanation: PDF: Automatically select the storage path priority: RDB >> Shared File System >> Local.

question: Regarding VM disk encryption in CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: It uses the AES-128-CBC algorithm and supports both RAW and QCOW2 disk formats
It uses kernel-level XTS-AES-256 symmetric encryption with the disk block device (RAW format) as the granularity and does not support QCOW2
Encryption is performed only inside the guest OS and is therefore dependent on whether the OS is Windows or Linux
Key information is stored only on the source host and is never migrated when the VM moves between hosts
explanation: PDF: Kernel-level disk encryption (XTS-AES-256 symmetric algorithm), unrelated to the type of VM operating system; encrypts by using the disk block device (RAW format) as the granularity; does not support the VM in disk file format (QCOW2). When the VM is migrated, key information and files are migrated synchronously.

question: Which of the following statements about the CAS computing resource hierarchy and its components are correct?
options: Computing resources of the CAS system are provided by the CVK host, which provides a physical hardware environment for the VM
A cluster is a computing resource collection composed of physical hosts and VMs
A host can be added to the host pool or the cluster
Only a host pool can contain VMs; clusters do not contain VMs
The VM template is a peer of the host and is managed outside the cloud resource hierarchy
explanation: PDF: Computing resources are provided by the CVK host; a cluster is a computing resource collection composed of physical hosts and VMs; a host can be added to the host pool or the cluster. D contradicts the cluster definition; E is wrong because the VM template is part of the cloud resource management.

question: Which of the following are virtual-disk cache modes supported by H3C CAS?
options: Direct read and write (directsync)
Level 1 physical cache (writethrough)
Level 1 virtual cache (none)
Level 2 virtual cache (writeback)
Level 3 virtual cache (deferred-sync)
explanation: PDF lists exactly four cache modes: directsync, writethrough (Level 1 physical cache), none (Level 1 virtual cache), and writeback (Level 2 virtual cache). There is no "Level 3 virtual cache / deferred-sync" mode.

question: Which of the following statements about the three CPU working modes in CAS are correct?
options: Compatibility mode is the recommended mode and uses the QEMU64 type
Host matching mode is poorer in migration compatibility compared with Compatibility mode
Straight-through mode delivers optimal performance but very poor migration compatibility
In Straight-through mode, different generations of CPUs from the same manufacturer may not be able to migrate
Compatibility mode provides hardware-level aes encryption acceleration to the VM
explanation: PDF: Compatibility (recommended) uses QEMU64; host matching is poorer in migration compatibility; straight-through has optimal performance but very poor migration compatibility, and different generations of CPUs from the same manufacturer may not be able to migrate. E is wrong: Compatibility cannot provide optimal performance such as aes encryption.

question: Which of the following are valid pre-allocation modes for a CAS virtual disk?
options: Thin
Zeroed
Lazy zeroed
Snapshot-zeroed
Sparse-deferred
explanation: PDF lists three pre-allocation modes: Thin (space allocated only when data is written), Zeroed (actual physical space allocated according to disk size), and Lazy zeroed (zeroed only when the write operation is performed for the first time). The other two are not in the source.

question: Which of the following statements about a CAS VM clone are correct?
options: The cloned VM is an exact copy of the original VM and is the same in operating system, application system, and data
The cloned VM and the original VM have the same IP address, so IP address conflict needs to be concerned
The clone operation generates a new vNIC MAC and UUID for the cloned VM
The clone operation requires the original VM to be powered off; running VMs cannot be cloned
Quick clone is suitable for virtual desktop scenarios
explanation: PDF: clone is an exact copy, generates new vNIC MAC and UUID, cloned VM has same IP (conflict risk), cloned VM can be in running or shutdown state (D is wrong), and quick clone is suitable for virtual desktop scenarios.

question: Which of the following are valid Backup Type options in the CAS VM backup configuration?
options: Full
Incremental
Differential
Snapshot-chain
explanation: PDF VM backup configuration lists Backup Type: Full, Incremental, Differential. "Snapshot-chain" is not a backup type.

question: Which of the following statements about ACL policy configuration in CAS are correct?
options: Supports inbound and outbound access control
Types include IPv4 and Layer 2
Supports ACL effective time setting
ACL policies operate at Layer 7 and inspect HTTP URL paths
ACL is the default action when both ACL and vFW are configured on a VM
explanation: PDF: ACL supports inbound and outbound access control; types include IPv4 and Layer 2; supports ACL effective time setting. D is wrong (ACL is L2/L3/L4, time-period based); E is wrong (when both are configured, vFW takes effect).

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS VM snapshot function, which is incorrect?
options: The snapshot function supports creation of snapshots of VMs in both shutdown and running states
When a snapshot is created on a running VM, whether to snapshot the memory can be chosen
A consistency snapshot requires CAStools to be installed and running on the VM
The VM disk format may be either Smart or any RAW block device for snapshot creation
explanation: Incorrect: PDF requires that the disk format of the VM must be Smart for snapshot creation; RAW block devices are not supported for snapshots.

question: Regarding offline VM migration in CAS, which of the following statements is wrong?
options: Offline migration means migrating the VM in a shutdown state
The image file must be stored in the shared storage when only the host changes
Migration may involve host change, storage change, or both host and storage change
Offline migration of a running VM is supported as long as the source and target hosts have identical CPUs
explanation: Wrong: PDF defines offline migration as migrating the VM in a shutdown state; offline migration of a running VM is by definition not offline migration.

question: Which of the following descriptions of full backup is incorrect?
options: It is a complete copy of all data and applications at a specific time point
It provides the best data protection
The amount of data to be backed up is very large and the consumed time is very long
It only copies data that has changed since the last full backup, reducing storage consumption
explanation: Incorrect: PDF says full backup is a complete copy of all data and applications; the description in D matches differential backup, not full backup.

question: Among the following descriptions of CAS disk encryption, which are incorrect? (Select two)
options: Disk encryption uses the XTS-AES-256 symmetric algorithm at the kernel level
It supports VM hot encryption; the data key is obtained from the kernel for decryption when starting
It supports the VM in the disk file format (QCOW2) as well as RAW block device format
It supports file-level encryption inside the guest operating system
When the VM is migrated within the cluster, key information and files are migrated synchronously
explanation: PDF explicitly states: "Do not support the VM in the disk file format (QCOW2)" and "Do not support the file-level encryption in the operating system." Both C and D contradict the source.

question: Regarding online hardware modification of a CAS VM, which of the following statements are wrong? (Select two)
options: vCPU supports online add; the online add of vCPU relies on support of the operating system
vRAM supports online add; the online addition of vRAM relies on support of the operating system
vNIC supports online add and online delete
vDisk supports online expand, add, and delete for all disk bus types regardless of driver
Online vCPU and vRAM can be added without any operating system support whatsoever
explanation: PDF: vDisk only supports online addition and online expansion of the Virtio disk (not all bus types), and vCPU/vRAM online add depends on OS support. So D and E are wrong.

question: Which of the following descriptions of incremental backup is incorrect?
options: It only backs up data that has changed since the last backup
There is no duplicate backup data and the backup time is short
Data recovery requires only the last incremental backup, with no need for the prior full backup
Data needs to be reversed one by one for recovery, which delays the recovery time
explanation: Incorrect: PDF says incremental backup recovery requires both the last full backup and the incremental backup, otherwise data cannot be recovered.

question: Regarding the CAS Trusted Zone for VM data-layer security, which of the following statements are incorrect? (Select two or three)
options: The trusted zone is a range of hosts in which VMs can be migrated
VMs within the protected security level can be migrated only between physical hosts in the trusted zone, and if no trusted zone is specified, migration is not allowed
When deleting a VM within the protected security level, it is mandatory to clear the physical bits of the disk file
The VM security level mark can be set and is separable from the VM at any time by the operator
VMs within the protected security level may be freely cloned as templates and have disks unloaded without restriction
explanation: PDF: the VM security level mark is inseparable from the VM, and operations such as clone, clone-as-template/convert-to-template, and disk unloading on VMs within the protected security level are NOT allowed. D and E contradict these rules.

question: Which of the following describes the entities that constitute the H3C CVM hot standby system?
options: Two active nodes and one arbitration node
An active node, a standby node, and an arbitration node
An active node, a standby node, and a witness storage
Two standby nodes synchronized via DRBD without a quorum
explanation: The PDF states: 'The hot standby system consists of three entities: an active node, a standby node, and an arbitration node.'

question: The H3C CVM hot standby function implements data synchronization between the active and standby servers by using which technology?
options: VMware vSphere Replication
DRBD storage replication solution
Synchronous LUN mirroring at the storage array
OCFS2 cluster file system journaling
explanation: The PDF states the hot standby data synchronization 'is implemented by using the DRBD storage replication solution.'

question: Regarding the H3C HA failure detection mechanism, which of the following is correct?
options: It is based on whether the CVM can ping the CVK host management IP
It is based on whether the CVK host can normally access the shared storage pool connected to the CVK host
It is based on whether the BMC of the host reports a hardware error via IPMI
It is based on whether the vSwitch flood-protection counter exceeds a threshold
explanation: The PDF states: 'The HA failure detection mechanism is based on whether the CVK host can normally access the shared storage pool connected to the CVK host.'

question: After a VM fails, the HA process on the CVK host tries to restart the VM locally. After how many consecutive detection cycles of failed restarts does it report to the CVM management node to migrate the VM?
options: One detection cycle
Two detection cycles
Three detection cycles
Five detection cycles
explanation: The PDF states: 'If the restart fails, after three consecutive detection cycles, a message is reported to the CVM management node to migrate the VM.'

question: Which of the following best describes FT (Fault Tolerance) on the H3C CAS platform?
options: A periodic snapshot-based recovery mechanism that reduces RPO to a few seconds
A technology for virtual machine failure switching that truly achieves continuous availability of applications, with no business downtime and no storage loss
An HA enhancement that reduces VM restart time to less than 30 seconds after a host failure
A storage-array-side replication that protects the boot volume of the primary VM
explanation: The PDF states FT 'is technology for virtual machines failures switching, truly achieving continuous availability of applications' with 'No business downtime and No storage loss.'

question: Which of the following descriptions of synchronous replication is correct?
options: An I/O write is released as soon as the master volume completes the write; the remote volume is updated on a snapshot schedule
An I/O write is released only after both the master volume and the remote volume return that the write operations are completed
Synchronous replication has the lowest data integrity but the highest performance because no acknowledgement is required from the remote
Synchronous replication is generally used over distances of 1000 km or more for cross-continent disaster recovery
explanation: The PDF states 'each I/O write operation is released only after both the master volume and the remote volume having the replication relationship return that the write operations are completed.'

question: Which of the following best describes the role of the SR-IOV NIC in H3C CAS?
options: It virtualizes a single physical NIC into multiple VF interfaces, each with a separate virtual PCIe channel, so each VM can directly access its own VF interface without coordinated intervention of the hypervisor
It bonds multiple physical NICs into a single logical NIC managed by the hypervisor vSwitch
It uses kernel bypass via DPDK polling threads to forward packets on behalf of all VMs
It inserts a software flow table between the vNIC and the physical NIC to enforce ACLs
explanation: The PDF states SR-IOV 'virtualize[s] a single physical NIC into multiple VF interfaces. Each VF interface has a separate virtual PCIe channel... so that the VM can directly access its own VF interface without the coordinated intervention of the hypervisor.'

question: Which of the following best describes the principle of the balloon driver memory over-commit method?
options: Slice the memory into 4 KB pages, deduplicate identical pages, and replace duplicates with pointers
Swap idle VM memory to a host disk file and remap it when accessed again
Use the balloon driver installed in H3C CAStools in the guest OS to reclaim memory and release the free memory in the VM to another VM that needs more memory on the same host
Reserve a fixed memory cap per VM and refuse allocations that would exceed it
explanation: The PDF states the balloon driver 'reclaim[s] the memory, and release[s], through expansion of the balloon driver, the free memory in the VM to a VM that needs more memory on the same host.'

question: In the H3C Active-Active Stretched Cluster best practice, what is the recommended maximum distance and network latency between the two data centers?
options: Distance not longer than 5 km and network latency not longer than 1 ms
Distance not longer than 100 km and network latency not longer than 10 ms
Distance not longer than 50 km and network latency not longer than 5 ms
Distance not longer than 10 km and network latency not longer than 2 ms
explanation: The PDF states for the Active-Active Stretched Cluster: 'Make sure the distance between two DCs is not longer than 5 km (3.11 miles) and the network latency is not longer than 1ms.'

question: Which of the following describes the monitoring content of computing and network DRS in H3C CAS?
options: Host memory, CPU, and network traffic
Disk I/O throughput, IOPS, and disk utilization
Power consumption, CPU temperature, and fan speed
Cluster network heartbeat, storage heartbeat, and BMC status
explanation: The PDF states for computing and network DRS: 'Monitoring content includes the host memory, the CPU, and the network traffic.'

question: Which of the following are configurable items of HA access control in H3C CAS?
options: Minimum number of effective nodes
Failover host
HA resource reservation
Heartbeat interval in milliseconds
Maximum number of FT pairs per host
explanation: The PDF lists HA access control items as: minimum number of effective nodes, failover host, and HA resource reservation.

question: Which of the following are prerequisites of the DPM policy in H3C CAS?
options: All hosts in the cluster must use the same shared storage
VMs in the cluster must use the same shared storage
VMs in the cluster must enable the Auto Migration function
Hosts in the cluster must enable the Wakeup function
explanation: The PDF DPM policy section lists all four prerequisites verbatim.

question: Which of the following statements about Application HA configuration prerequisites are correct?
options: The HA function must be enabled on the cluster where the VM is located
The CASTools must be correctly installed in the operating system of the VM
The compatibility list of applications needs to be checked, and applications not in the compatibility list need to be properly adapted and verified
The VM must have direct hardware passthrough enabled via SR-IOV
Storage DRS must be enabled on the cluster
explanation: The PDF Application HA configuration page lists exactly these three prerequisites: HA enabled on cluster, CASTools installed in the VM OS, and application compatibility verified.

question: Which of the following are VMs-to-Hosts rule relationships that can be set in H3C CAS?
options: Must Run on hosts
Should Run on hosts
Must Not Run on hosts
Should Not Run on hosts
May Run on hosts only during maintenance
explanation: The PDF lists exactly these four VMs-to-Hosts relationships: Must Run, Should Run, Must Not Run, Should Not Run.

question: Regarding asynchronous remote replication, which of the following statements are correct?
options: It is generally performed periodically and the consistency of data between replicated volumes cannot be guaranteed
After a local master volume completes a write, a snapshot is created and then replicated to the remote volume
It has relatively low requirements on the bandwidth and distance
It provides the highest level of data integrity among replication modes
It releases each I/O write only after the remote volume acknowledges the write
explanation: The PDF asynchronous replication section explicitly states (1) periodic execution without guaranteed consistency, (2) snapshot-then-replicate principle, and (3) low bandwidth/distance requirements.

question: Which of the following are host wakeup methods supported by H3C CAS DPM?
options: Network wakeup (Wake-On LAN)
IPMI wakeup (command to the BMC)
Serial console power-on signal
PXE remote boot
explanation: The PDF host wakeup section lists exactly two methods: Network wakeup (configured via BIOS Wake-On LAN) and IPMI wakeup (IPMI command to BMC).

question: Which of the following statements about VM affinity rule configuration are correct?
options: VM rules include 'VMs must be on the same host' and 'VMs cannot be on the same host'
Association actions between VMs include startup, shutdown, and startup/shutdown
At least two VMs need to be included in the VM rules
A VM can be constrained by two types of non-conflicting VM rules at the same time
At most one VM rule may apply per cluster
explanation: The PDF VM affinity rule configuration page lists exactly these four statements.

question: Among the following descriptions of the H3C CAS HA heartbeat, which is incorrect?
options: The network heartbeat checks whether the HA process of the CVK node in the management network runs normally
The storage heartbeat checks whether the CVK host in the storage network can normally access the shared storage pool connected to the CVK host
After detecting the storage heartbeat anomaly, the HA module verifies the result three times before triggering migration
The network heartbeat operates over the BMC IPMI interface independent of the management network
explanation: The PDF defines the network heartbeat as a check over the management network, not via BMC/IPMI; IPMI is only used for DPM host wakeup.

question: Regarding H3C synchronous replication, which of the following statements is wrong?
options: It has the highest level of data integrity among replication methods
Performance is reduced due to the delay in transmitting data between arrays
It is generally used in short-distance scenarios such as the banking system (10 KM to 100 KM, local)
It tolerates large data loss because it batches writes asynchronously to the remote array
explanation: Synchronous replication is described as 'almost intolerable to data loss' and requires immediate acknowledgement from the remote volume; it does not batch writes asynchronously.

question: Among the following descriptions of H3C transparent page sharing memory over-commit, which one is incorrect?
options: It uses 4 KB as the unit to slice the memory
It reduces redundant memory pages of the host to one page and replaces other pages with pointers
When a VM tries to write a shared memory page, a dedicated page is replicated for modification
It swaps idle pages to a disk swap partition and remaps them when accessed again
explanation: Swapping to a disk file is the memory-swapping method, not transparent page sharing; transparent page sharing uses page deduplication with copy-on-write.

question: Among the following statements about H3C SRM homogeneous disaster recovery, which is wrong?
options: SRM relies on the replication function of third-party storage arrays for data synchronization
The SRA plug-in for auto-switching is provided by storage manufacturers
SRM supports automatic switching with storage of ONEStor, 3PAR, Primera, Nimble and MacroSAN
SRM provides RPO=0 with continuous synchronous block replication between the two sites
explanation: The PDF SRM section describes RPO at minute level using asynchronous remote replication, not RPO=0 with synchronous replication. RPO=0 is the property of the Active-Active Stretched Cluster solution, not SRM.

question: Regarding H3C DRS, which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: Storage DRS monitors disk I/O throughput, IOPS, or disk utilization
Computing and network DRS monitors host memory, CPU, and network traffic
DRS continuously monitors the utilization of the computing resource pool and storage resource pool in the cluster
The DRS Duration parameter is the interval for checking the host status
The DRS threshold defines the maximum allowed CPU temperature before migration is triggered
explanation: Duration is the maximum time the monitoring content is allowed to continuously exceed the threshold (not the check interval, which is the 'Interval' parameter), and DRS thresholds are utilization-based, not CPU-temperature-based.

question: Among the following descriptions of H3C HA access control parameters, which are incorrect?
options: Minimum number of effective nodes: if the number of hosts normally running in the cluster is less than this parameter, the VM cannot perform HA fault migration
Failover host: the migration destination host is preferentially selected from the specified host group, which cannot be used as destination for adding or migrating the VM
HA resource reservation: reserve a specific percentage of resources for HA, and when remaining cluster resources are less than the set ratio, VMs in the cluster can no longer be started
Failover host group always permits new VM creation and ad-hoc migration into it during normal operation
If HA resource reservation is exceeded, additional FT pairs are automatically created to compensate
explanation: The PDF explicitly states the failover host group 'cannot be used as the destination host for adding or migrating the VM', so option 3 contradicts it. There is no FT-pair-creation behavior tied to HA resource reservation in the source.

question: Among the following descriptions of the H3C cloud rainbow online migration prerequisites, which are wrong?
options: The version of the remote data center CVM to be added is the same as that of the local CVM
The IP address type of the cluster joined to migrate the host where the VM is located is the same as that of the cluster joined to migrate the destination host
The local data center and the remote data center must each be configured
The remote and local CVMs may run on different major versions as long as the storage arrays are identical
Cloud rainbow migration requires SR-IOV passthrough to be enabled on both the source and destination CVK hosts
explanation: The PDF explicitly requires the remote CVM version to be the same as the local CVM, contradicting option 3. SR-IOV passthrough is not listed as a cloud rainbow prerequisite anywhere in the source.

question: Which of the following commands is used to obtain a list of clusters managed by the HA process on a CAS host?
options: cha cluster-list
cha node-list cluster-id
cha cluster-status cluster-id
cha node-status host-name
explanation: The PDF states that 'cha cluster-list' is used to obtain a list of clusters managed by the HA process; the other commands query host lists, cluster status, or node status.

question: Which of the following commands is used to query a vSwitch and its port information on a CVK host?
options: ovs-vsctl -V
ovs-appctl bond/show
ovs-vsctl show
ovs-vsctl list-ports
explanation: Per the PDF, 'ovs-vsctl show' queries the vSwitch and its port information. 'ovs-vsctl -V' queries the internal version of OVS, and 'ovs-appctl bond/show' queries vSwitch port binding information.

question: Regarding the four alarm severity indicators displayed in the upper-right of the CAS main management interface, which statement is correct?
options: The four levels are Emergency, Critical, Major, and Notice.
The four levels are Critical Alarm, Major Alarm, Minor Alarm, and Warning.
The four levels are Fatal, Severe, Warning, and Information.
The four levels are Alert, Critical, Warning, and Debug.
explanation: The PDF explicitly lists the four alarm indicators on the main management interface as Critical Alarm, Major Alarm, Minor Alarm, and Warning.

question: Which of the following commands is used to query vSwitch port binding (bond) information on a CVK host?
options: ovs-vsctl bond-show
ovs-appctl bond/show
ovs-vsctl show bond
ovs-appctl port/bond
explanation: The PDF documents 'ovs-appctl bond/show' as the command to query vSwitch port binding information.

question: Which of the following statements about using virsh to start/shut down VMs in the background of a CVK host is correct?
options: virsh is the primary recommended way to shut down VMs during routine operations.

It is recommended to normally shut down the VM from inside the VM operating system; virsh start/shutdown should only be used in the background when the VM cannot be operated in the foreground under special circumstances.
virsh start/shutdown must always be used before any foreground shutdown is attempted.
virsh list --all only displays VMs that are currently powered on.
explanation: The PDF notes that VMs should normally be shut down from inside the guest OS, and virsh start/shutdown in the background should be used only when the VM cannot be operated in the foreground under special circumstances.

question: Regarding CASToolkit and iService, which of the following statements is correct?
options: CASToolkit replaces iService entirely; iService is deprecated in V4.0.
CASToolkit is executed only on CVK nodes; iService runs locally on the CVM and never uploads any data externally.
CASToolkit is an automatic inspection tool that includes two parts: executing CASToolkit on the CVM node, and uploading the result to the iService platform to get a report.
iService is the tool executed at the CVM node, and CASToolkit is the cloud platform that receives reports.
explanation: The PDF says CASToolkit is an automatic inspection tool with two parts: executing the CASToolkit tool on the CVM node and uploading the result to the iService platform to get a report.

question: Which of the following statements about the CPU model 'Host Passthrough mode' in CAS performance optimization is correct?
options: Host Passthrough mode virtualizes physical CPUs of different models into vCPUs of the same model for best migration compatibility.

Host Passthrough mode directly passes the host CPU model and major functions to the VM, provides the best performance for guest OSs, but VMs can only be migrated between hosts with exactly the same CPU model.
Host Passthrough mode virtualizes a physical CPU into the most similar vCPU model and provides good migration compatibility.
Host Passthrough mode is the default mode and is recommended for clusters with mixed CPU generations.
explanation: The PDF states Host Passthrough mode directly passes the host CPU model and major functions to the VM, providing the best performance but poor migration compatibility, with migration only possible between hosts with exactly the same CPU model.

question: Which of the following channels can be configured under Alarm Management / Notifications in CAS to receive alarms timely?
options: Email
SMS
Syslog
SNMP Trap
explanation: The PDF states that under Alarm Management / Notifications you can configure Email/SMS/Syslog to receive alarms timely. SNMP Trap is not listed as a notification channel in this module.

question: Which of the following are types of files included in the decompressed CAS log compressed package?
options: Logs of CVK hosts
HA configuration file information
Logs of Web container Tomcat
Logs of CVM management platform functions
Source code patches of CAS modules
explanation: The PDF lists the decompressed log file types as logs of CVK hosts, HA configuration file information, logs of Web container Tomcat, and logs of CVM management platform functions. Source code patches are not part of the log package.

question: Which of the following statements about CPU model configuration options in CAS are correct?
options: Compatible mode features good migration compatibility but cannot provide the best CPU performance for guest OSs.
Host mode virtualizes a physical CPU into the most similar vCPU model and provides poor migration compatibility but better performance than Compatible mode.
Host Passthrough mode features the best migration compatibility across mixed-CPU clusters.
Compatible mode virtualizes physical CPUs of different models into vCPUs of the same model.
Host mode delivers identical performance to Compatible mode but with stricter migration constraints.
explanation: Per the PDF, Compatible mode unifies different physical CPUs into the same vCPU model for good migration but reduced performance; Host mode virtualizes to the most similar vCPU model with poor migration but better performance. Host Passthrough has poor migration compatibility, not best.

question: According to the PDF, which of the following steps are part of the recommended procedure for replacing the CPU, memory, or disk hardware on a CAS host?
options: Check the cluster health and check the service load
Migrate VMs and back up management platform data
Enter maintenance mode and shut down the host
Skip the cluster status check after the replacement to save time
After replacement, exit maintenance mode, check cluster status, and perform one-key inspection
explanation: The PDF's hardware-replacement workflow includes checking cluster health and service load, migrating VMs, backing up management platform data, entering maintenance mode, shutting down the host, replacing the hardware, exiting maintenance mode, checking cluster status, and one-key inspection. Skipping the post-check is never recommended.

question: Regarding the Modify Time and Clock procedure for the CVM/host, which of the following statements are correct?
options: Time may be modified into the future but not into the past.
Before modifying time, back up CVM configuration and check whether time-sensitive applications exist.
Before the modification, turn off all VM time synchronization functions and turn off the VMs.
It is safe to roll the system clock backward without further checks.
After modifying the time of the NTP server / CVK host, start the VMs and restore the service.
